Adam Farence: Data was collected from the Chicago Cook County recorder of deeds, ranging from January 1, 2018 to May 20th, 2026. The "LISF_ALL" tab includes every piece of data collected in raw form—no changes to that data have been made. The "LISF_municipal_liens_removed" tab has the same data, but with foreclosure filings initiated by local government removed. As per Sam Lounsberry, these filings aren't true foreclosures. The government makes them to collect on unpaid items, such as taxes. There's no intent to actually take the property, which separates these from other lenders.
